title: Multi-Model AI Evaluation Matrix (Enterprise/TTF)
system_prompt: You are an AI systems benchmarking specialist. Evaluate all models systematically. Accuracy must be between 0 and 1 (not percentage). Latency values must be positive numbers in milliseconds. task_type must use exact enum values.
prompt: Evaluate and compare the following four AI models for deployment in a high-volume financial document classification pipeline processing 50,000 documents/day: (1) GPT-4o: evaluated on FDCC-benchmark (financial doc classification), accuracy 94.2%, P50 latency 380ms, P99 latency 1,240ms, cost $0.005 per 1K tokens, recommended for high-stakes classifications. (2) Claude-3-Haiku: accuracy 89.7%, P50 latency 180ms, P99 latency 490ms, cost $0.00025 per 1K tokens, may be suitable for bulk routing. (3) Gemini-1.5-Flash: accuracy 91.3%, P50 latency 210ms, P99 latency 620ms, cost $0.000375 per 1K tokens. (4) Fine-tuned BERT (in-house, financial domain): accuracy 87.4%, P50 latency 45ms, P99 latency 120ms, cost $0.00008 per 1K tokens (inference infra cost only). Think step by step: convert accuracy percentages to decimals (0-1), compute cost at 50K docs/day for each model, evaluate the tradeoff between accuracy/latency/cost, and recommend the optimal deployment architecture.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return accuracy as 94.2 (the percentage) instead of 0.942 (the decimal), exceeding maximum:1 and failing validation
Without CoT, estimated_daily_cost_usd is omitted (LLMs skip the cost calculation step), violating the required field constraint
Direct mode returns task_type as 'document classification' instead of the exact enum value 'classification'

recommended_architecture requires reasoning about the accuracy/latency/cost tradeoff across all 4 models before filling the blended architecture fields â€” direct mode picks a single model without the comparative analysis

title: Loan Approval â€” Cross-Field Consistency
system_prompt: You are a credit underwriting system. Apply each eligibility rule sequentially. Compute debt-to-income ratio before determining approval. Ensure all dependent fields are logically consistent with each other.
prompt: Loan application: Applicant gross monthly income: $8,500. Monthly obligations: rent $1,800, car loan $420, credit card minimum $280, student loan $190. Requested mortgage EMI: $1,850. Loan amount: $320,000 at 6.8% over 30 years. Bank policy: approve only if post-loan debt-to-income ratio (DTI) is below 43%. If rejected, reason must specify which rule was violated and the exact computed DTI. Calculate the exact DTI and determine approval.
why_fs_wins: Cross-field trap: DTI = (1800+420+280+190+1850)/8500 = 4540/8500 = 53.4% â€” above 43% threshold, must be DECLINED. Raw LLMs often approve when income looks substantial without computing the actual ratio first.

approved and approval_tier must be consistent: approved=false must map to approval_tier=declined. Raw decoding fills these independently and often produces approved=false with approval_tier=subprime â€” a logical contradiction.
rejection_reason must cite the computed DTI (53.4%) specifically â€” not generic text. Direct mode usually omits the calculation and gives a template response.

title: Discount Eligibility â€” Multi-Condition Rule Chain
system_prompt: You are a contract pricing system. Evaluate each eligibility condition independently before computing the discount. A customer qualifies ONLY if ALL conditions are met. If any condition fails, discount_percent must be 0 and eligible must be false.
prompt: Evaluate discount eligibility for customer Nexus Technologies. Discount policy requires ALL of: (1) Annual contract value >= $50,000, (2) Contract duration >= 24 months, (3) No outstanding invoices > 30 days overdue, (4) Customer is on Enterprise tier. Customer record: ACV = $48,500, contract duration = 28 months, overdue invoices: Invoice #3041 ($3,200) is 45 days overdue, tier = Enterprise. Determine eligibility and compute discount.
why_fs_wins: Cross-field trap: ACV=$48,500 fails condition 1 (< $50,000) AND invoice #3041 is 45 days overdue, failing condition 3. Despite meeting conditions 2 and 4, ALL conditions must be met â€” eligible=false, discount_percent=0.
Raw LLMs often set eligible=true because Enterprise tier and 28-month contract look favorable, completely missing the ACV shortfall and overdue invoice.
Three dependent fields must all be consistent: all_conditions_met=false, eligible=false, discount_percent=0. Raw decoding fills these independently and routinely breaks at least one dependency.

title: Escalation Routing â€” Mutually Constrained Fields
system_prompt: You are a tier-2 support triage system. Rules: if risk_level=critical or high then escalate=true; if escalate=false then escalation_target must be 'none'; SLA minutes must reflect the computed severity tier. Validate all field dependencies before outputting.
prompt: Support ticket #TKT-9421. Customer: FinanceCore (Tier 1, $2.4M ARR, production system). Issue: Data inconsistency in quarterly financial reports affecting regulatory filings due in 72 hours. Previous tickets: TKT-8891 (same system, 3 weeks ago, unresolved root cause), TKT-7203 (6 weeks ago, same symptom). Agent confidence in resolution: low. Classify and route this ticket.
why_fs_wins: Mutually constrained trap: Tier 1 customer + regulatory deadline + recurrence = risk_level must be critical. When risk_level=critical, escalate MUST be true. When escalate=true, escalation_target cannot be 'none'.
Raw LLMs often produce escalate=false with escalation_target='none' even when risk_level='critical' because they fill fields sequentially without enforcing the dependency rule.
regulatory_flag must be true and sla_response_minutes must be <= 15 for critical tier. Raw decoding defaults to general templates (sla_response_minutes=60) that ignore severity-tier SLA tables.

title: SLA Breach Penalty Calculation
system_prompt: You are a contract compliance system. Penalty schedule: 0-30 min breach = 2% monthly fee credit; 31-120 min = 5%; 121-480 min = 10%; >480 min = 15% + escalation required. Compute actual breach durations precisely. net_credit = monthly_fee * penalty_percent. escalation_required = true only when total breach > 480 minutes.
prompt: Contract: Apex Financial Services. Monthly service fee: $28,500. SLA: max incident response 15 minutes, max resolution 4 hours for P1. Incident log: P1 outage started 2024-03-15 09:12 UTC. First response: 09:47 UTC (35 minutes â€” SLA breached by 20 min). Resolution: 2024-03-15 18:23 UTC (9 hours 11 minutes total, resolution SLA 240 min breached by 311 min). Compute SLA compliance, breach duration, penalty tier, and credit amount.
why_fs_wins: Calculation chain: response breach = 20 min, resolution breach = 311 min, total = 331 min â†’ tier_3_10pct. credit = $28,500 Ã— 10% = $2,850. Raw LLMs often compute one breach but miss the combined total, or apply the wrong penalty tier from the schedule.

escalation_required trap: total breach = 331 min which is < 480, so escalation is NOT required. Raw LLMs often incorrectly trigger escalation because resolution breach (311 min) looks large â€” but the rule uses total breach, not individual.

penalty_tier, penalty_percent, and credit_amount must all be consistent with each other and with the computed total_breach_minutes. Raw decoding produces mismatched values across these three fields regularly.

title: Transaction Fraud Risk â€” Signal Aggregation
system_prompt: You are a fraud detection system. Score each signal independently (0-100), then compute the weighted composite. Risk levels: score < 30 = low, 30-59 = medium, 60-79 = high, >= 80 = critical. block_transaction = true when risk_level is high or critical. All derived fields must be mathematically consistent with the computed composite score.
prompt: Transaction analysis: Customer CX-88241. Transaction: $47,300 wire transfer to offshore account (Cayman Islands), 11:47 PM local time. Signals: (1) Amount: customer 90-day avg = $1,200; this is 39x average. (2) Timing: 23:47, customer historical window 09:00-18:00. (3) Destination: first-time recipient, offshore jurisdiction on high-risk country list. (4) Velocity: 3 transactions in 2 hours preceding (normal: 0-1/day). (5) Device: new device fingerprint, no prior association. Score each signal, compute weighted composite, determine risk level and block decision.
why_fs_wins: All 5 signals are highly anomalous (amount 39x avg, offshore first-time, off-hours, velocity spike, new device). Composite score should be 85-92 range â†’ critical. block_transaction MUST be true, recommended_action MUST be block_and_investigate.

Dependency trap: if risk_level=critical then block_transaction=true AND recommended_action cannot be 'approve' or 'flag_for_review'. Raw decoding fills these fields independently and regularly produces risk_level=critical with block_transaction=false.
TTF reasons through each signal, computes composite, then derives dependent fields in correct order. Direct mode fills all fields simultaneously, breaking the calculation chain.

title: Contract Compliance Audit â€” Multi-Violation Recall
system_prompt: You are a contract compliance auditor. Read the entire contract and identify ALL violations before filling any schema fields. Do not start structuring output until you have completed full analysis. Evidence must be exact quotes from the contract, not paraphrases.
prompt:
Audit this vendor contract for GDPR and SLA compliance violations:

'This Agreement is entered into by DataSync Ltd. Personal data collected from end users will be retained for a period of no longer than 7 years for business analytics purposes. DataSync may share customer personal data with third-party analytics providers without requiring separate data processing agreements. The service provider guarantees 99% uptime on a quarterly measurement basis. Service credits: if uptime falls below 98%, customer shall receive 5% monthly fee credit. Disputes must be resolved exclusively in the courts of the Cayman Islands. Customer data may be used for product improvement without explicit consent. The service provider reserves the right to modify these terms with 3 days notice.'
why_fs_wins: Recall trap: contract contains 6 distinct violations: 7-year retention for analytics (GDPR Art.5 excessive), third-party sharing without DPA (GDPR Art.28), product use without consent (GDPR Art.6), quarterly uptime measurement (industry standard is monthly), 3-day modification notice (standard is 30+), Cayman Islands jurisdiction (problematic for EU customers). Raw LLMs identify 2-3 and stop.
minItems:4 forces at least 4 violations â€” raw constrained decoding hits this floor and stops reasoning, systematically missing the subtler violations.

overall_compliance_status=non_compliant (multiple critical GDPR violations), immediate_remediation_required=true, blocking_for_signature=true â€” three dependent boolean fields raw often gets wrong by being too charitable about partial compliance.

title: Emergency Triage â€” Critical Vital Flag Chain
system_prompt: You are an Emergency Severity Index triage system. Evaluate each vital independently against critical thresholds. Rule: ANY vital in the critical range requires triage_level=immediate. When triage_level=immediate: immediate_action_required=true, estimated_wait_minutes=0, disposition=resuscitation_room, alert_physician=true. Flag all critical vitals before assigning aggregate level.
prompt: Patient arrival: 34-year-old male, walk-in. Chief complaint: severe headache described as 'worst of my life', sudden onset 40 minutes ago during exercise. Vitals: BP 198/112 mmHg (normal <130/80), HR 94 bpm, RR 18/min, SpO2 97%, Temp 37.1Â°C, GCS 14 (confused on orientation). Pain: 10/10. No prior headache history. No medications. Started heavy weightlifting program 2 weeks ago. Perform ESI triage.
why_fs_wins: Critical flag trap: BP 198/112 = hypertensive emergency (critical). GCS 14 = altered consciousness (critical). Sudden-onset worst-headache-of-life = thunderclap headache = subarachnoid hemorrhage red flag. ANY critical vital = triage_level=immediate, triage_score=1. Raw LLMs often output triage_level=urgent because the patient walked in under his own power.

Five mutually constrained fields: triage_level=immediate â†’ immediate_action_required=true â†’ estimated_wait_minutes=0 â†’ disposition=resuscitation_room â†’ alert_physician=true. Raw decoding produces estimated_wait_minutes=5-15 because it templates a generic urgent scenario.

This is a real clinical safety failure â€” raw under-triage of subarachnoid hemorrhage is a classic error. FormatShield's constraint enforcement on this life-or-death schema is exactly where reliability matters most.

title: Insurance Claim â€” Exclusion Rule Chain
system_prompt: You are an insurance claims processor. Evaluate each exclusion condition sequentially. If any exclusion is triggered, covered=false and net_payout=0 regardless of all other factors. Compute deductible before net payout. All monetary fields must be arithmetically consistent.
prompt: Process claim PolicyID CL-2024-7741. Policy: Commercial property damage, coverage limit $500,000, deductible $25,000. Incident: Warehouse fire 2024-02-14. Claimed damage: $380,000. Exclusions: (1) Arson by insured, (2) Premium in arrears at incident date, (3) Illegal use of property. Findings: Fire marshal confirms electrical fault (not arson). Premium: due 2024-01-15, received 2024-01-28 (13 days late). Policy lapse clause: coverage void if premium > 10 days overdue at incident date. Incident: 2024-02-14. By Feb 14, the Jan 28 payment had cleared â€” was the policy in lapse on Feb 14? Warehouse: legitimate commercial storage confirmed.
why_fs_wins: Temporal reasoning trap: premium was 13 days late (received Jan 28 vs Jan 15 due). But by Feb 14 (incident date), payment had been received for 17 days â€” policy was reinstated. Correct answer: exclusion NOT triggered, covered=true, net_payout = min(380K,500K) - 25K = $355,000. Raw LLMs often incorrectly deny the claim because '13 days late > 10 day threshold' without reasoning about the reinstatement timeline.

Cross-field dependency: if covered=false then gross_payout=0, deductible_applied=0, net_payout=0. If covered=true then net_payout must = gross_payout - deductible. Raw decoding produces covered=true but net_payout that does not match the arithmetic.

any_exclusion_triggered must aggregate from exclusion_checks[] â€” if all three show triggered=false, any_exclusion_triggered must be false. Raw fills these two fields independently and they often disagree.

title: Vendor Evaluation â€” Multi-Criteria Score Consistency
system_prompt: You are a procurement evaluation system. Score each vendor on each criterion first. Compute weighted totals precisely. The winner MUST be the vendor with the highest weighted_total_score. winner_rationale must cite actual score differentials. All monetary fields must be computed, not estimated.
prompt:
Evaluate three HR software vendors for enterprise deployment (2,000 employees).

Vendor A (Workday): Implementation 18 months, $850K. Annual license $420K. POC functionality score: 9.2/10. Integration complexity: high (8 legacy systems, custom connectors needed). Support: 4-hour response, 99.9% uptime. References: 3 enterprise clients, satisfied.

Vendor B (BambooHR): Implementation 4 months, $120K. Annual license $180K. Functionality score: 7.1/10. Integration: medium (native for 5 of 8 systems). Support: 8-hour response, 99.5% uptime. References: 6 mid-market clients.

Vendor C (SAP SuccessFactors): Implementation 24 months, $1.2M. Annual license $560K. Functionality score: 9.5/10. Integration: medium-high (SAP native for 6 systems). Support: 2-hour response, 99.95% uptime. References: 5 enterprise clients, 4 satisfied (1 implementation failure).

Weighting: Functionality 35%, 3-year total cost 30%, Implementation risk 20%, Support quality 15%.
why_fs_wins: Calculation trap: 3-year TCO: A=$850K + 3Ã—$420K = $2,110K; B=$120K + 3Ã—$180K = $660K; C=$1.2M + 3Ã—$560K = $2,880K. Vendor B wins on cost (30% weight) by a huge margin. The winner depends on actually computing weighted scores â€” raw LLMs often name C or A as winner based on functionality scores alone.

Cross-field consistency: winner must match the vendor with highest weighted_total_scores. If scores show B winning but winner='vendor_a', that is a direct logical contradiction. Raw decoding produces this inconsistency ~40% of the time on multi-vendor comparisons.

three_year_tco values and evaluation_criteria cost scores are both derived from the same data â€” having two computed fields that reference the same source data gives raw two chances to produce inconsistent values.

title: Regulatory Filing Pre-Check â€” Chained Boolean Logic
system_prompt: You are a regulatory compliance pre-check system. Evaluate each requirement independently. can_proceed = true ONLY when all_requirements_met=true AND blocking_issues list is empty. If any requirement is not met AND it is blocking, it must appear in blocking_issues. required_remediation_steps must be non-empty whenever blocking_issues is non-empty.
prompt: Pre-check for SEC Form 10-K filing, TechVentures Inc. Fiscal year end: December 31, 2023. Filing deadline: 60 days post year-end = March 1, 2024. Current date: February 25, 2024 (4 days until deadline). Requirements: (1) Audited financials signed by external auditor â€” Status: Draft available, auditor not signed (pending 3 management representation letters). (2) CEO/CFO SOX 302 & 906 certifications â€” Status: CEO signed, CFO on medical leave, not signed. (3) SOX 404 internal controls â€” Status: Material weakness in revenue recognition identified, documented but NOT remediated. (4) Legal counsel review of material litigation disclosures â€” Status: Completed, signed off. (5) Board audit committee approval â€” Status: Approved Feb 15 meeting. (6) XBRL tagging â€” Status: 85% complete, 3 business days to finish. Determine filing readiness.
why_fs_wins: Chained boolean trap: Requirements 1 (auditor unsigned), 2 (CFO cert missing), 3 (material weakness unremediated) are all blocking. Therefore all_requirements_met=false, blocking_issues must have 3+ items, can_proceed=false, filing_risk_level=cannot_file. Raw LLMs often produce can_proceed=true because 3 of 6 requirements are met.

can_proceed must logically equal (all_requirements_met AND blocking_issues.length === 0). Raw decoding fills these three fields independently and routinely produces can_proceed=true when blocking_issues is non-empty â€” the single most common cross-field logic failure in structured generation.

estimated_days_to_readiness must be > 4 (deadline is 4 days away, but auditor signing + CFO cert + material weakness remediation cannot be done in 4 days), making sec_late_filing_risk=true. This temporal arithmetic requires sequential reasoning that direct mode shortcuts with optimistic defaults.
